skill.name: nano-banana-2
skill.description: Generate images with Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview (Nano Banana 2) via inference.sh CLI. Capabilities: text-to-image, image editing, multi-image input (up to 14 images), Google Search grounding.
skill.summary: Generate images with Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview via inference.sh CLI.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 43 patterns but all are false positives. External command detections (32) are markdown code blocks showing example CLI usage, not actual code execution. Network detections (5) are documentation URLs and example JSON. Weak crypto detections (6) have no actual cryptographic code. The skill safely wraps the inference.sh CLI with constrained bash access (infsh * only).

content.user_title: Generate images with Google Gemini 3.1 Flash
content.value_statement: Create stunning images from text descriptions using Google's latest Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview model. Supports text-to-image generation, image editing, multi-image input, and real-time Google Search grounding for current information.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview
Edit existing images by providing input images and modification prompts
Accept up to 14 input images for multi-image analysis and generation
Set output resolution up to 4K quality
Enable Google Search grounding for real-time information in generated images
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and authentication
Limited to Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview model only
Cannot generate images without network access to inference.sh platform
Maximum 14 input images for multi-image operations

use_cases.title: Marketing Content Creation
use_cases.description: Generate product visuals, social media images, and marketing materials from text descriptions.

use_cases.title: Image Editing and Enhancement
use_cases.description: Modify existing images by describing desired changes like adding elements or changing styles.

use_cases.title: Visual Prototyping
use_cases.description: Quickly generate visual concepts and variations for design exploration and client presentations.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image with the prompt: a banana in space, photorealistic

prompt_templates.title: Custom Aspect Ratio
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a panoramic mountain landscape with northern lights in 16:9 aspect ratio
prompt_templates.scenario: Generating images with specific dimensions for presentations or social media

prompt_templates.title: Image Editing
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this image to add a rainbow in the sky, maintaining the original style and quality
prompt_templates.scenario: Modifying existing images with targeted changes

prompt_templates.title: Real-Time Grounded Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an artistic visualization of current weather in Tokyo with Google Search enabled
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating images based on real-time information from the web

output_examples.input: Generate a futuristic cityscape at sunset with flying cars
output_examples.output: Returns generated image(s) showing a vibrant sunset scene over a futuristic city with flying vehicles, rendered in photorealistic style

output_examples.input: Edit the uploaded landscape photo to add a rainbow in the sky
output_examples.output: Returns the edited image with a naturally integrated rainbow arching across the sky, preserving the original photo quality and lighting

output_examples.input: Create 4 variations of a minimalist coffee shop logo design
output_examples.output: Returns 4 distinct minimalist logo concepts featuring coffee-related imagery in clean, modern design styles

best_practices: Use specific, descriptive prompts with style, lighting, and composition details for better results
Start with lower resolution (1K) for testing prompts, then increase to 4K for final outputs
Enable Google Search grounding when generating images that need current information or real-world accuracy
anti_patterns: Do not use vague prompts like 'make something nice' - be specific about subjects, styles, and mood
Avoid requesting copyrighted content, real person likenesses, or sensitive imagery
Do not expect photorealistic results from artistic style prompts - match prompt language to desired output style

faq.question: What is Nano Banana 2?
faq.answer: Nano Banana 2 is a skill that provides access to Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview model through the inference.sh platform for AI image generation.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need the inference.sh CLI (infsh). Run npx skills add inference-sh/skills@agent-tools for installation instructions.

faq.question: How many images can I generate at once?
faq.answer: You can generate multiple images in one request using the num_images parameter. You can also provide up to 14 input images for editing or multi-image analysis.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: Input images support JPEG, PNG, and WebP formats. Output format can be specified using the output_format parameter.

faq.question: What does Google Search grounding do?
faq.answer: When enabled, Google Search grounding allows the model to access real-time information from the web, useful for generating images related to current events, weather, or news.

faq.question: Can I edit existing images with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, provide existing image URLs in the images array along with a prompt describing the desired changes. The model will edit the images while preserving their style and quality.
